evals.name: explicit-autopsy-request
evals.prompt: Can you do a deep autopsy of how I've been using claude code? I've been at it heavily for the past few months and the built-in /insights feels too surface level. I want actual scores on where I'm strong and where I'm weak, backed by numbers from my own sessions. HTML report is fine.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs all 4 phases, produces HTML at ~/.claude/usage-data/cc-user-autopsy.html containing 8 rule-based scores, a personalized peer review with session-ID evidence, and 14 charts. Final message names the top improvement area and points to the HTML path.

assertions.name: skill-invoked
assertions.text: The agent invoked the cc-user-autopsy skill (visible by running aggregate/sample/build_html scripts or mentioning phases).

assertions.name: html-file-exists
assertions.text: The file ~/.claude/usage-data/cc-user-autopsy.html was written and is >= 20000 bytes.

assertions.name: peer-review-has-three-strengths-three-improvements
assertions.text: The HTML contains both 'Three things you are doing well' and 'Three specific improvements' headings.
check_file_contains_all: Three things you are doing well

assertions.name: eight-dimensions-present
assertions.text: The HTML shows all 8 scoring dimensions (D1-D8).

assertions.name: charts-rendered-14
assertions.text: The HTML contains exactly 14 <canvas> elements and 14 Chart.js instantiations (growth curve + 13 others).

assertions.name: final-message-points-to-report
assertions.text: The final message to the user mentions the HTML file path so they can open it.

assertions.name: does-not-dump-full-report-inline
assertions.text: The final message does not paste the full HTML or full peer review into the conversation — just a short summary.

evals.name: implicit-workflow-audit
evals.prompt: I feel like I've been grinding on claude code but not sure what I'm actually doing well. Is there a way to look at my session data and tell me if there are bad habits I've picked up?
evals.expected_output: Skill is triggered even though the user didn't explicitly say 'autopsy' or 'insights'. HTML report is produced and the agent highlights bad habits backed by specific numbers.

assertions.name: skill-triggered-on-implicit-phrasing
assertions.text: The agent recognized this as a workflow-audit request and invoked the cc-user-autopsy skill (rather than doing ad-hoc greps).

assertions.name: html-file-exists
assertions.text: The file ~/.claude/usage-data/cc-user-autopsy.html was written and is >= 20000 bytes.

assertions.name: improvement-has-actionable-fix
assertions.text: At least one improvement in the peer review contains 'Actionable fix' pointing to a specific daily action.

assertions.name: response-mentions-bad-habits
assertions.text: The final message explicitly names at least one habit the user should change.

evals.name: hr-portfolio-request
evals.prompt: I'm planning to apply to AI companies like Anthropic, OpenAI, and xAI. Can you build me an honest portfolio report of my Claude Code usage that a hiring manager would actually read? I want it to show what I've shipped and where I'm genuinely strong — not a marketing pitch.
evals.expected_output: Skill invoked with --audience hr. HTML contains the at-a-glance profile card, shipped-with-claude section, how-to-read primer, and growth curve. The agent explicitly acknowledges the portfolio framing.

assertions.name: hr-flag-used
assertions.text: The agent ran build_html.py with --audience hr.

assertions.name: hr-html-exists
assertions.text: An HR-variant HTML file was produced (name may vary).

assertions.name: profile-card-rendered
assertions.text: HTML contains the at-a-glance profile card.

assertions.name: how-to-read-primer-present
assertions.text: The 30-second primer is embedded.

assertions.name: growth-curve-chart
assertions.text: The composite growth curve chart is rendered.

assertions.name: response-acknowledges-hr-framing
assertions.text: The agent mentions the portfolio / hiring manager framing in its response (not just dumps the report).

evals.name: specific-stat-query-should-not-over-trigger
evals.prompt: Quick question: how many claude code sessions have I run in the last 30 days? Just the number.
evals.expected_output: Skill should NOT trigger — this is a one-stat question that can be answered with a single grep/ls, not a holistic review.

assertions.name: skill-not-triggered
assertions.text: The cc-user-autopsy skill was NOT invoked (no aggregate.py / sample_sessions.py / build_html.py calls).

assertions.name: no-html-generated
assertions.text: No new cc-user-autopsy.html was written.

assertions.name: answer-is-concise
assertions.text: The answer is a single number or a short sentence, not a multi-section report.
